Are You Looking to Build a Career That Contributes to a Brighter Future?
• Own delivery of accurate and timely accounting and financial close for assigned countries in accordance with accounting policies, procedures, and reporting timetables
• Ensure integrity, completeness, and accuracy of financial records, trial balances, and statutory accounts
• Perform and lead detailed reviews of accounts, trial balances, and balance sheet reconciliations, including preparation of draft variance commentary in OneStream
• Prepare and review local statutory accounts and required statistical reporting
• Act as primary point of contact for group, statutory, and tax audits; lead audit preparation, respond to queries, and resolve findings
• Execute and oversee complex accounting activities, including accrual calculations, estimates, and judgment-based postings
• Perform Corporate Income Tax (CIT) and Deferred Tax (DT) processes in line with the tax manual and applicable regulations
• Ensure effective execution of internal controls and review controls performed by junior team members
• Initiate tax and dividend payment requests in accordance with governance and authorization frameworks
• Prepare ad hoc financial reporting and analysis for internal stakeholders
• Prepare, maintain, and review process documentation to ensure consistency, compliance, and knowledge continuity
• Lead mentoring, onboarding, and training of team members; provide functional guidance and quality reviews
• Drive continuous improvement initiatives and proactively identify risks, control gaps, and efficiency opportunities
• Collaborate closely with colleagues across GBS, the wider Finance organization, and internal and external stakeholders
• Deputies for the Manager when required, ensuring continuity of delivery, controls, and decision-making

What Are We Looking For?
• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Commerce, or related field; professional qualification (CA, CPA, ACCA, CIMA) is an advantage
• 7–10 years of progressive experience in accounting, financial control, or statutory reporting
• Strong experience in entity-level accounting, statutory reporting processes, audits, and tax processes (CIT and DT), preferably within a multi-country or centralized finance environment
• Strong IFRS foundation with demonstrated ability to interpret and apply local statutory requirements within a controlled governance framework
• Experience with ERP systems and consolidation/reporting tools (e.g., OneStream)
• Advanced MS Excel skills and strong analytical capability
• Ability to apply sound professional judgment to complex accounting and control matters
• Strong command of English (written and verbal), with the ability to communicate clearly with global stakeholders and produce professional documentation.
• Experience working in a shared services, centralized finance, or multinational environment is an advantage.

Do You Want to Be Part of a Global Team Where Collaboration Drives Success?
• At Hempel, we believe in a customer-centric approach while maintaining operational efficiency.
• Your ability to balance both will play a crucial role in ensuring smooth transactions and exceptional service delivery.
• From day one, your input and initiative will be welcomed and expected.
• In return, you’ll have the opportunity to grow within a global company that values collaboration, trust, and career development.

Does This Sound Like a Match?

Click the ‘Apply for position’ button and submit your CV and application letter in English via our recruitment system.

Application due

2026-03-15

Seniority Level

Mid-Senior level

Job Functions

Accounting / Auditing, Finance

Industry

Chemicals

At Hempel, you’re welcomed to a global community of +7000 colleagues around the world. A workplace built on shared trust, mutual respect and support for each other.

We’re on a mission to double our impact. To succeed, we need bright minds and team players. Which is why, from day one, your input, ideas and initiative are not only welcomed, but expected. In return, you will enjoy great opportunities for development in a growing global company – and be part of the solution by contributing to our global impact.

At Hempel, we are committed to everyone feeling safe, valued and treated equally, in an environment where each employee can bring their authentic selves to work.  We believe that inclusion is key to innovation and by bringing together the most diverse perspectives and talents, we can achieve great things, together.

Walk-in Interview Drive | Chartered Accountant (CA) - 1-3 Years Experience

About Juniper Green Energy Limited:
We are amongst the top 10 largest renewable IPPs in India in terms of total capacity* as of December 31, 2024. We develop, build, operate, and maintain projects through our in-house engineering, procurement, construction, operations & maintenance teams.

Our portfolio of projects includes wind, solar, wind-solar hybrid and firm and dispatchable renewable energy including battery energy storage systems. As of May 31, 2025, our total portfolio stands at approximately 10,069 MWp spread over 48 projects across four states in India.

Location: Juniper Green Energy Limited,
4th Floor, Building 4, Candor TechSpace,
Sector 48, Gurugram – , Haryana
Walk-in Dates: 30th March 2026 to 2nd April 2026
⏰ Time: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM
Experience: 1–3 years

Job Responsibilities:

Project Finance & Debt Management:
• Assist in financial modelling and project feasibility analysis
• Support loan documentation, disbursement tracking, and compliance
• Coordinate with banks/NBFCs for project funding
• Monitor debt servicing (EMIs, interest payments, covenants)
• Track utilization of funds and ensure compliance with sanction terms.

Financial Reporting:
• Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
• Assist in audits and ensure compliance with accounting standards
• MIS reporting and financial analysis

Accounts Payable:
• Manage vendor invoices, verification, and processing
• Ensure timely payments and reconciliation of vendor accounts
• Handle TDS deductions and other compliance

Treasury:
• Monitor daily cash flow and fund position
• Manage banking transactions and relationships
• Assist in fund planning and working capital management

Indirect Tax
• Computation of monthly GST liability
• Preparing Input Service Distributor (ISD) working
• Filing monthly GSTR-1, GSTR-6 and GSTR-3B
• Preparing ITC reconciliation between GSTR-2A/2B and books
• Preparing GST tax invoice, Export Invoice, Delivery challan, Bill of Supply etc.
• Issue E-way bill
• Preparing annual returns (GSTR-9 and 9C)
• Preparing reconciliations between books and GST records
• Filing GST amendments, new GST registrations etc.
• Stay updated with changes in indirect tax laws

Required Skills:
• Qualified CA upto 3 years of experience
• Strong understanding of accounting principles
• Good analytical and Excel skills
• Attention to detail and problem-solving mindset
• Good communication skills
Preferred:
• Internship/articleship exposure in relevant areas
• Familiarity with ERP systems

⚠ Important Note:
Candidates who have applied or interviewed with us in the last 6 months are requested to skip this walk-in drive.

Role Overview
This is a unique opportunity to join the India Shared Services Centre of one of the UK's fastest-growing private healthcare groups.
As a Financial Analyst – Corporate Finance, you will drive high-impact strategic and financial initiatives ranging from capital structuring and fundraising to M&A and growth planning. You'll work closely with the UK Head of Corporate Finance and collaborate across business units and support teams.
While this is a full-time, in-office role at our Pune Shared Services Centre, your reporting manager is based in the UK. You will therefore be expected to work with a high degree of independence, manage your own priorities, and communicate proactively with senior stakeholders across time zones.
This role is ideal for someone with a strong technical foundation in finance, a commercial mindset, and the ambition to scale with a global organisation.
Key Responsibilities
Corporate Finance & Investment Strategy
• Advise on and support external corporate finance transactions including equity and debt fundraising, property financing, and M&A.
• Conduct business case evaluations using DCF, IRR, and sensitivity models for strategic investment decisions.
• Build and maintain advanced financial models to assess capital deployment, returns, and valuation.
• Prepare investor-grade presentations and reports for the Board, investors, and senior stakeholders.
• Support exit preparedness and stakeholder communications in future financing rounds.
FP&A Support & Business Partnering
• Work closely with the FP&A team to track centre-level profitability and margin expansion initiatives.
• Act as a finance partner to UK centres, supporting cost initiatives, pricing analysis, and strategic planning.
• Assist in the development of dashboards, KPIs, and unit economics frameworks for operational improvement.
• Prepare monthly performance reviews and collaborate with clinical and commercial teams to enhance financial visibility.
Strategic Initiatives
• Contribute to cross-functional strategic projects, including service expansion, new partnerships, and geographic growth.
• Bring analytical insight to complex financial problems involving multiple stakeholders.
What Success Looks Like in Six Months
• Independently handling UK stakeholder requests.
• Delivering 2–3 high-quality financial models or analyses.
• Contributing to at least one strategic initiative (M&A / expansion / fundraising).
Skills & Experience Required
Must-Have
• 4–8 years of experience in corporate finance, corporate development, investment banking, FP&A, or consulting
• Strong command over financial modeling, valuation, and capital structuring concepts
• Excellent written and verbal communication, including ability to create polished presentations and reports
• Highly organized with strong analytical and commercial acumen
• Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent priorities and deliver under tight timelines
• Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills
• A self-starter with intellectual curiosity and a commercial mindset, comfortable operating in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment
• A CA, CA (Inter), or MBA (Finance) qualification is preferred, though not mandatory for exceptionally strong candidates.
Preferred
• Healthcare sector experience is strongly preferred
• Prior exposure to private equity, or services industries
• Hands-on experience working with UK/Europe-based clients
• Knowledge of group financial reporting, FP&A tools (e.g., Power BI, Anaplan)
Working Conditions
• Office environment in Pune (WFO).
• Standard corporate workweek, with occasional extended hours during deal closings or month-end reporting.
• Collaborative team setting; regular interaction with global finance colleagues and cross-functional business units.
Please answer the qualifying questions carefully — incomplete or casual responses may lead to your application not being considered.
If you’re ready to take ownership, sharpen your expertise, and make a real impact in global healthcare finance, apply today and grow with us!
Pay: ₹2,000,000.00 - ₹2,600,000.00 per year
Benefits:
• Health insurance
Application Question(s):
• How many total years of experience do you have in Corporate Finance, FP&A, Investment Banking, or Consulting? Please include a brief breakdown by role type.
• Please describe the most complex financial model you have built independently — including the type of model, its purpose, and your specific role in building it.
• Name the most senior audience you have presented financial analysis to. What was the key message you were communicating and what was the outcome of that presentation?
• Have you worked on any M&A, fundraising, or capital markets transactions? If yes, please describe your specific tasks and the stage of the transaction you were involved in.
• In your most recent finance role, approximately what percentage of your time involved hands-on financial modelling? Were the models built independently or in support of someone else's work?
• Are you comfortable with a full-time WFO role at our Sinhgad Road, Pune office in the UK shift (12 PM – 9 PM IST)?
• Please mention your current area of residence in Pune.
• What is your current CTC (fixed + variable, in LPA) and your expected CTC? Please note that applications without this information cannot be processed further.
• What is your notice period and your earliest possible joining date?
Work Location: In person

At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.

We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.

Join EY and help to build a better working world.

Senior- FTS And V (Process/TOM)

EY – GDS – Consulting – Finance

As part of our EY - Finance Consulting Team, you will help clients develop their Performance Improvement initiatives and identify potential opportunities for performance improvements drive them. This includes techno-functional role conducting finance processes assessments, deep diving and analysing pain areas and coming up with solutions. The client base spans across industries and countries.

The opportunity

We’re looking for Consultant / Sr. Consultant with expertise in Process Assessment assignments with process mapping experience. Also, experience in FP&A, Financial Accounting and Reporting domain is an added advantage to join the global EY - Finance Consulting team. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of a leading firm whilst being instrumental in the growth of a new service offering.

Your Key Responsibilities

• Experience with finance functions – ‘Record to report’, ‘Procure to pay’ and ‘Order to cash’ processes involving account reconciliations, Journals, Transaction matching, Variance analysis, intercompany and Fixed assets accounting / management
• Conduct workshops with clients to perform current state As-Is process assessment and design & optimize Future state processes as per leading practices & industry benchmarks
• Identify process improvement opportunities using automation, technology upgradation & process standardization, Finance Transformation and Operation model design and Assessments
• Create L1-L5 “As-Is” and “To-Be” process mapping using tools such as Planview, Nimbus, ARIS, IBM Blueworks and Visio
• Experience in implementation and development of process mapping tools for business functions
• Design KPIs and Control frameworks for O2C, P2P and R2R processes
• Strong accounting skills and understands financial reports and statements
• Knowledge on Financial Consolidation, Cost Allocation, Finance Planning and Budgeting Processes
• Should have knowledge of activities in financial close of an organisation for month / Quarter / Annual close periods
• Good Knowledge on ledger and sub-ledger systems; technical know-how of SAP ERP / HANA, Oracle GL (good to have)
• Translate business requirements to technical language and model (good to have)
• Good experience in Chart of accounts (CoA) design
• Managing/ supervising teams of people on project work
• Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks in a high-pressure
• Open to travel (Domestic & International)

Skills And Attributes For Success

• Consulting experience is a good to have
• Confident & professional communication style
• Proficiency in English (oral and written)
• Ability to clearly articulate messages to a variety of audiences
• Flexible and adaptable; able to work in ambiguous situations
• Able to work effectively at all levels in an organization
• Capability to influence others and move toward a common vision or goal
• Must be a team player and able to work collaboratively with and through others
• Strong analytical skills as well as excellent problem-solving skills for interpreting the IFRS standards

To qualify for the role, you must have

• CA, CFA, CPA, MBA OR Master’s in business administration in Finance
• 1-2 years (for Staff level) and 3-6 years (for Senior level) of work experience in Finance domain
• Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills

Ideally, you’ll also have

• Exposure to tools like ERP, HFM, Visio, ARIS, BlueWorks, Celonis, Alteryx, etc.
• Project management skills, stakeholder management
• Finance Transformation

What We Look For

• A Team of people with commercial acumen, technical experience and enthusiasm to learn new things in this fast-moving environment
• Opportunities to work with EY Advisory practices globally with leading businesses across a range of industries

What Working At EY Offers

At EY, we’re dedicated to helping our clients, from world’s top companies — and the work we do with them is as varied as they are. You get to work with inspiring and meaningful projects. Our focus is education and coaching alongside practical experience to ensure your personal development. We value our employees and you will be able to control your own development with an individual progression plan. You will quickly grow into a responsible role with challenging and stimulating assignments. Moreover, you will be part of an interdisciplinary environment that emphasizes high quality and knowledge exchange. Plus, we offer:

• Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around
• Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career
• The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you

EY | Building a better working world

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.

data.job_description:
Role Overview
We are looking for a highly capable Accounting Professional to manage financial statements, lead closing activities, and handle day-to-day finance operations in SAP.
This role will function as a key support to the Financial Controller, acting as their right hand in ensuring accurate reporting, operational efficiency, and financial control across the organization.

Key Responsibilities
• Manage end-to-end financial closing activities (monthly, quarterly, and year-end)
• Prepare and review financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) with high accuracy
• Perform daily finance and accounting operations in SAP (FICO), including postings, reconciliations, and issue resolution
• Ensure timely and accurate execution of routine accounting activities and transactions
• Act as a key support to the Financial Controller in all finance-related matters
• Assist the Controller in reporting, analysis, and decision support
• Perform account reconciliations and investigate discrepancies proactively
• Maintain and ensure integrity of financial data within SAP
• Coordinate with auditors and support internal/external audit processes
• Ensure compliance with accounting standards, internal controls, and statutory requirements
• Drive improvements in closing cycles, reporting accuracy, and finance processes

Key Requirements
• 5–10 years of experience in accounting and financial reporting
• Strong experience in managing monthly, quarterly, and year-end closings independently
• Hands-on experience and strong competencies in SAP FICO with strong exposure to daily transactional activities
• Proven ability to manage both operational accounting and reporting responsibilities
• Strong understanding of financial statements and accounting principles (IFRS/GAAP/local standards)
• Ability to work closely with senior stakeholders, especially supporting a Financial Controller
• High attention to detail with strong ownership and accountability
• Ability to work under tight deadlines and manage multiple priorities
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
• Excellent communication and coordination skills - fluency in english (written + spoken), conversational proficiency in German is preferred.

Preferred Qualifications
• CA / CPA / ACCA or equivalent qualification
• Experience in manufacturing or multi-entity environments
• Exposure to SAP S/4HANA
• Experience working in a fast-paced, process-driven organization

The role is responsible for end-to-end tax compliance, accounting close support, and inter-company transaction management for APAC operations. The position ensures timely statutory compliance, accurate financial reporting, robust reconciliations, and audit readiness, while working closely with internal stakeholders, banks, and external consultants.
Responsibilities
• Finalize and file GSTR-1, GSTR-2, GSTR-3B, Annual Returns, and LUT within statutory timelines.
• Perform GST turnover reconciliation, handle GST payments, challan uploads, approvals, and utilization entries.
• Manage ISD returns, input reclassification entries, and GST TDS reconciliation.
• Prepare MIS/Dashboards for GST reviews.
• Pass TDS accounting entries and prepare TDS computations.
• Coordinate for quarterly TDS return filings and processing advance tax payments.
• Perform inter-company reconciliations, knock-offs, and receivable tracking.
• Handling BOE, AWB, and invoice documentation.
• Execute SAP cockpit runs, resolve I-Doc failures.
• Supporting monthly and quarterly close activities related to inter-company transactions.
• Posting tax provisions, reclassification entries, GL balance transfers, etc.
• Preparing month-end schedules and supporting monthly/quarterly accounting close in Blackline and SAP.
• Preparing audit schedules and reconciliations.
• Compiling and providing GST, TDS, advance tax, and inter-company data.
• Supporting preparation of Clause 34 and Clause 44 Tax Audit Reports.

Qualifications
• Education: 1st Attempt or 2nd Attempt Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) only.
• Work Experience : 5-8 years with Indirect Taxation (GST), Direct Tax (TDS, Advance Tax), Intercompany Accounting.
• Hands-on: Experience working in SAP, Blackline, GST Portal, Advanced Excel .
• Must have: Experience working with Big4 / external consultant is preferred

**Selected intern's day-to-day responsibilities include**:
1. Handling the accounting entries in Tally Prime /other accounting software
2. Working on bookkeeping and auditing
3. Working on statutory audits, internal audits, etc.
4. Assisting in maintaining documentation and filings
5. Filing of income-tax, TDS, and GST returns
6. Working on ledger scrutiny and finalization of accounts
7. Working on the audit of various types of organizations
8. Handle company incorporation and ROC work

As the Business Head Finance at the FMCG Distribution Company, your role will be crucial in driving financial discipline, profitability, governance, and sustainable growth across the business. You will work closely with the Directors and leadership team to provide strategic financial insights and support crucial business decisions. Your responsibilities will include the following key areas:

- Partnering with Directors and business leaders to drive profitability, cost optimization, and growth strategy
- Providing financial insights to support pricing, margins, channel mix, and route-to-market decisions
- Leading annual business planning, long-range forecasting, and scenario analysis
- Evaluating new business opportunities, expansions, distributor models, and investments

In terms of financial planning and control, you will be responsible for:

- Owning budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis across regions and functions
- Monitoring key financial KPIs such as gross margin, contribution margin, EBITDA, ROI, and cash flow
- Strengthening internal controls to minimize leakage, pilferage, and inefficiencies
- Ensuring timely and accurate MIS for management and board reporting

Your role will also involve managing working capital, cash flow, compliance, audit, governance, team leadership, capability building, and stakeholder management.

Qualifications required for this position include:

- Chartered Accountant (CA) / MBA (Finance) mandatory
- 7-15 years of progressive finance leadership experience
- Strong experience in FMCG distribution, trade finance, or consumer goods
- Proven exposure to multi-location operations and distributor-led models
- Hands-on experience with systems and financial automation
- Deep understanding of distribution economics
- Expertise in working capital and cash flow management
- Knowledge of industry standards, compliance regulations, and corporate finance best practices

- Monitor credit portfolio performance and identify areas of concern.
- Develop and implement credit risk mitigation strategies.

- Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.

In addition to the core job responsibilities and qualifications, the company offers a competitive salary and benefits package, opportunities for career advancement, a collaborative and supportive work environment, and medical insurance.

As a Manager / Senior Manager in the finance team at Pune, your role will involve handling end-to-end Accounting operations, Financial reporting, Audits, Taxation, and Regulatory compliance. You should have 10-12 years of post-qualification experience and be a Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) with expertise in Indian accounting standards, tax laws, and regulatory frameworks. Proficiency in financial systems and accounting software such as Tally ERP, SAP, Zoho Books, QuickBooks, etc., along with advanced MS Excel and financial analysis skills are required. Your leadership, team management, analytical, and communication skills will be crucial for this role.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Lead end-to-end accounting functions including bookkeeping, general ledger, AR/AP management, reconciliations, and cash flow management.
- Prepare and review financial statements in compliance with Indian Accounting Standards (IGAAP/Ind AS).
- Oversee budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and performance reporting.
- Manage all direct and indirect tax compliances, including Income Tax, GST, TDS, and Advance Tax.
- Oversee preparation and filing of statutory returns (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B, GSTR-9, TDS returns, IT returns).
- Ensure compliance with ROC filings, FEMA regulations, and other statutory requirements.
- Liaise with statutory, internal, and tax auditors to ensure smooth audit processes.
- Prepare audit schedules, support audit activities, and drive timely closure of audit findings.
- Build strong working relationships with internal stakeholders, external consultants, clients, auditors, and regulatory authorities.
- Lead initiatives for process automation, standardization, and operational efficiency improvements.
- Mentor and guide junior team members; ensure knowledge sharing and professional development within the team.

Job Description:

Ecolab is seeking an FP&A Director responsible for leading financial planning and analysis activities across the organization. This role serves as a key point of contact for Division Finance Managers and enables them to drive best‑in‑class financial performance and business insights. The position requires strong partnerships across multiple stakeholders and offers significant exposure and growth opportunities. As FP&A Director, you will work closely with divisional finance teams to drive results and strengthen financial rigor.

What’s in it for You?
• Join a fast-growing organization offering a competitive salary and benefits.

• Ensure delivery of services in alignment with established service-level agreements (SLAs) with divisions.

• Collaborate closely with division finance leaders to help FP&A managers understand internal and external business dynamics and their impact on financial outcomes.
• Lead and coordinate the development of annual plans, ensuring strong collaboration among all key stakeholders.
• Oversee monthly close and forecast processes—including providing direction, reviewing results, supporting system loads, and preparing content for senior leadership business reviews.

• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI

As the world works and lives faster, FIS is leading the way. Our fintech solutions touch nearly every market, company and person on the planet. Our teams are inclusive and diverse.
